execution_schedule.batch_rationale: Foundation validation and routing tasks can execute in parallel—no shared dependencies or file modifications. T1 validates fixture structure independently; T2 tests workflow routing independently.

uows.rationale: Verify fixture validation—foundational task for ensuring malformed input fails fast per NFR. No dependencies; can start immediately. Complexity: simple.

Fix workflow routing test assertion and verify synthetic mode detection—foundational task required before integration testing. No dependencies; runs in parallel with T1. Includes fixing test_main_defaults_to_bundled_synthetic_fixture assertion. Complexity: simple.

execution_schedule.batch_rationale: Intake artifact creation logically follows foundation work (T1, T2). T3 depends on validated input handling and correct routing—both verified in batch 1. Must complete before downstream stage verification (T4).

uows.rationale: Verify intake agent creates canonical artifacts for synthetic stories—critical gate for AC2 and AC3. Depends on T1 (validation) and T2 (routing). Exercises intake agent behavior with synthetic prompt. Complexity: moderate. High risk: intake agent behavior is not yet verified.

execution_schedule.batch_rationale: Downstream stage verification requires canonical artifacts created in batch 2. T4 is explicitly dependent on T3. Verifies that ADO operations are correctly skipped for synthetic fixtures—critical for AC3.

uows.rationale: Verify downstream stages skip ADO operations for synthetic artifacts—required for AC3 verification. Depends on T3 (intake artifacts). Tests task-gen, task-assigner, software-engineer, and QA stage behavior. Complexity: moderate. High risk: downstream stage synthetic mode detection is not yet verified.

execution_schedule.batch_rationale: Integration test exercises full workflow (intake → planning → assignment → implementation → QA). Must wait for batch 3 to ensure all components are verified independently. Depends on T1 (validation), T2 (routing), T3 (artifacts). End-to-end validation gate.

uows.rationale: Add comprehensive integration test for full synthetic workflow—validates AC1, AC2, AC3 work together. Depends on T1, T2, T3. Creates test_full_synthetic_workflow() in test_steps_and_run.py. Complexity: moderate. High risk: requires all prior components to be working correctly.

execution_schedule.batch_rationale: Documentation task summarizing verified behaviors. Runs last after all validation and testing complete. Depends on T2 (routing) and T3 (artifacts). Low priority; supports constraint 'easy to verify'.

uows.rationale: Document synthetic workflow usage and behavior—explains how to run with synthetic fixtures, differences from ADO mode, custom fixture creation, error handling. Depends on T2, T3. Complexity: simple. Priority: low. Synthesizes findings from T1-T5.

critical_path_rationale: Critical path is determined by the longest dependency chain: T1 (or T2) → T3 → T4 → T5. Path length is 4 batches (or 3 if we count only T1→T3→T5, excluding T4). T4 is on critical path because it must verify downstream behavior before final integration test can confirm no regressions. T2 is in parallel with T1, so not extending critical path.

risk_ordered_items.risk_reason: Test assertion fix (test_main_defaults_to_bundled_synthetic_fixture) must be correct or entire workflow routing will fail. If this test assertion is wrong, subsequent tests will fail incorrectly.
risk_ordered_items.mitigation: Scheduled in batch 1 for early de-risking. Simple task—should complete quickly.

risk_ordered_items.risk_reason: Intake agent behavior is unknown—agent must correctly interpret synthetic-mode prompt and create proper canonical artifacts. If intake artifacts are malformed, all downstream stages fail.
risk_ordered_items.mitigation: Scheduled in batch 2 (immediately after routing verified). Careful artifact validation in DoD. Creates clear failure diagnostics.

risk_ordered_items.risk_reason: Downstream stages must correctly detect synthetic mode and skip ADO operations. If ADO operations are accidentally triggered, workflow is broken. Current state of downstream stages is unverified.
risk_ordered_items.mitigation: Scheduled in batch 3 (immediately after intake artifacts verified). Unit tests with mocked ADO calls. Integration test confirms no real API calls.

risk_ordered_items.risk_reason: Integration test is gate for all three acceptance criteria. If integration test fails, the entire feature is broken. High complexity; exercises all components.
risk_ordered_items.mitigation: Scheduled in batch 4. Depends on all prior verification tasks (T1, T2, T3, T4). Clear failure diagnostics in test DoD.

batch_1_parallelization.safety_rationale: T1 (fixture validation) and T2 (workflow routing) have no shared file modifications, no interdependent logic, and can be merged cleanly. Both are unit-level tests with no side effects. Parallelization is safe and recommended.

estimated_total_duration: Moderate—5 sequential batches but with 2 parallelized tasks in batch 1. Actual duration depends on intake agent implementation time and downstream stage verification completeness. Expect iterations if intake artifacts don't match expected schema or if downstream stages need additional synthetic mode detection.

notes.fixture_context: All tasks operate on synthetic_story.json fixture. Fixture is well-formed, validated, and ready for workflow execution. No fixture changes needed.
notes.artifact_preservation: Batch 2 (T3) is critical gate—intake artifacts must match canonical schema and preserve raw fixture input. All downstream tasks depend on correct artifact structure.
notes.synthetic_mode_detection: Batch 3 (T4) validates that downstream stages correctly detect synthetic mode by checking for absence of ado_provenance metadata. This is architectural requirement for AC3.
notes.test_assertion_fix: T2 requires fixing test_main_defaults_to_bundled_synthetic_fixture assertion to expect runner='claude' parameter. This is listed as concrete DoD item. Simple fix, high priority.
notes.integration_test_scope: T5 integration test must run all stages end-to-end: intake → task-gen → task-assigner → software-engineer → QA. Each stage must complete without errors.
notes.no_production_changes: Story notes confirm no production code changes required—infrastructure is already in place. All tasks are verification, testing, and documentation.

librarian_queries.query: What is the current structure of agent-runner and where are intake handlers?
librarian_queries.answer_summary: From tasks.yaml: agent-runner has run.py entry point, workflow_inputs.py for input resolution, steps.py for stage orchestration. Synthetic support infrastructure already in place with load_story_fixture() validation, resolve_workflow_input() routing, build_intake_prompt() synthetic mode generation.

metacognitive_context.decision_rationale: Scheduling follows the task story's recommended sequential order (T1/T2 → T3 → T4 → T5 → T6) while maximizing safe parallelization (T1, T2 in parallel in batch 1). This ordering balances risk de-risking (high-risk items T2, T3, T4 scheduled early) with dependency respect (all explicit task dependencies honored). Batching strategy groups logically related work: foundation (batch 1), artifact creation (batch 2), downstream verification (batch 3), integration (batch 4), documentation (batch 5). Critical path identifies longest dependency chain (4 batches including T4 verification).

alternatives_discarded.approach: Aggressive parallelization: run T3, T4, T5 in parallel after T1/T2
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: Violates explicit dependencies. T4 depends on T3; T5 depends on T1, T2, T3. Additionally, T4 and T5 have overlapping concerns (both verify downstream behavior), so sequential execution prevents duplicate work.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Skip T4 (downstream verification) and run T5 integration test to cover all stages
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: T4 provides focused unit-level verification of downstream stage skip logic, which is architectural requirement for AC3. T5 is end-to-end but doesn't necessarily test all edge cases T4 covers. Both are valuable.
Story constraint 'Keep any code changes minimal and easy to verify' requires clear documentation. T6 is explicitly in task list and maps to AC1, AC3. Must be included.

knowledge_gaps: Exact behavior of intake agent when processing synthetic-mode prompt—will be clarified during T3 execution
Current state of downstream stages' synthetic mode detection—will be clarified during T4 execution
Whether additional error handling beyond current validation is needed—will be clarified during T1 execution
metacognitive_context.scheduling_confidence_rationale: Very high confidence due to: (1) explicit task definitions with clear dependencies, (2) detailed story notes with recommended sequential ordering, (3) clear acceptance criteria mapping, (4) documented knowledge gaps and risks. Confidence is not 10/10 only because downstream stage behavior (T4) and intake agent behavior (T3) are not yet empirically verified—but this is why T3 and T4 are scheduled early for de-risking.
